title: try / except - when things go wrong
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: Code that might fail goes in try; the response to failure goes in except. The program recovers instead of crashing.
explanation: Some things you can't prevent - bad input, a missing key, dividing by zero. try lets you attempt anyway and catch the fall. The except block runs only if something in try raised an error. It's how a program meets the unexpected without falling apart - which, come to think of it, is a decent thing to learn in general.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: 6 / 2 is normal division - remember what / always returns.
6 / 0 would crash, but except catches it and returns 0.

exercises.question: When does the except block run?
options: only if the try block raised an error
hints: If try finishes cleanly, except is skipped.
It runs only if something in try raised an error.

exercises.question:
Code that might fail goes in which block?

_:
    risky()
